revision.text:
'''白水鉱泉'''（しらみずこうせん）は、[[大分県]]中南部の[[由布市]][[庄内町 (大分県)|庄内町]]阿蘇野にある[[鉱泉]]である。日本では珍しい天然[[炭酸水]]の鉱泉として有名。
[[九重山|九重連山]]東部に位置する黒岳（標高1,586m）の山麓にあり、黒岳への登山口でもある。「白水」という名は、鉱泉に含まれる多量の二酸化炭素のために、周囲の岩や土壌が白く染まり、湧き出す水が白く見えることに由来する。
鉱泉は、「ラムネ水」とも呼ばれて飲用とされ、砂糖を加えると[[サイダー]]に似た味となる。また、入浴施設もあり、冷泉と鉱泉を沸かした湯とに入浴できる。源泉温度は、8 - 9℃で、糖尿病、胃のむかつき、肝臓病、アトピー・皮膚病、便秘に効能があるとされる。
西方約2kmには、[[名水百選]]に選定された[[男池湧水群]]が位置している。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.oitarian.jp/mizu/16.html 庄内町の白水（しらみず）鉱泉 - 大分県観光ポータルサイト: : おおいたリアンプラス] * [http://www.coara.or.jp/report/hakken/oita/syounai/report/siramizu/ 白水鉱泉 - 大分再発見-暮らしに役立つ地域情報サイト]

[[九重山|九重連山]]東部に位置する[[黒岳 (大分県)|黒岳]]（標高1,586m）の山麓にあり、黒岳への登山口でもある。「白水」という名は、鉱泉に含まれる多量の二酸化炭素のために、周囲の岩や土壌が白く染まり、湧き出す水が白く見えることに由来する。
鉱泉は、「ラムネ水」とも呼ばれて飲用とされ、砂糖を加えると[[サイダー]]に似た味となる。また、入浴施設もあり、冷泉と鉱泉を沸かした湯とに入浴できる。源泉温度は、8 - 9℃で、糖尿病、胃のむかつき、肝臓病、アトピー・皮膚病、便秘に効能があるとされる。
西方約2kmには、[[名水百選]]に選定された[[男池湧水群]]が位置している。

{{No footnotes|date=2019年5月}} '''白水鉱泉'''（しらみずこうせん）は、[[大分県]]中南部の[[由布市]][[庄内町 (大分県)|庄内町]]阿蘇野にある[[鉱泉]]。日本では珍しい純粋な天然[[炭酸水]]の鉱泉（単純[[二酸化炭素泉]]）として有名である。
[[九重山|九重連山]]東部に位置する[[黒岳 (大分県)|黒岳]]（標高1,586 [[メートル|m]]）の山麓にあり、黒岳への登山口でもある。「白水」という名は、鉱泉に含まれる多量の二酸化炭素のために、周囲の岩や土壌が白く染まり、湧き出す水が白く見えることに由来する。
鉱泉は、「ラムネ水」とも呼ばれて飲用とされ、砂糖を加えると[[サイダー]]に似た味となる。また、入浴施設もあり、冷泉と鉱泉を沸かした湯とに入浴できる。源泉温度は、8 - 9℃で、糖尿病、胃のむかつき、肝臓病、アトピー・皮膚病、便秘に効能があるとされる。
西方約2 [[キロメートル|km]]には、[[名水百選]]に選定された[[男池湧水群]]が位置している。

revision.comment: 白水鉱泉とどのような関係があるのか不明

revision.text:
{{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water}} '''白水鉱泉'''（しらみずこうせん）は、[[大分県]]中南部の[[由布市]][[庄内町 (大分県)|庄内町]]阿蘇野にある[[鉱泉]]。日本では珍しい純粋な天然[[炭酸水]]の鉱泉（単純[[二酸化炭素泉]]）として有名である<ref name="visit-oita">{{cite web |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=白水（しらみず）鉱泉 |work=日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト |publisher=[[ツーリズムおおいた]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{cite web |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=国立公園へ出かけよう！ くじゅう_おすすめコース 男池・白水鉱泉コース |publisher=環境省 |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
[[九重山|九重連山]]東部に位置する[[黒岳 (大分県)|黒岳]]（標高1,586 [[メートル|m]]）の山麓にあり<ref name="visit-oita" />、黒岳への登山口でもある<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=くじゅう連山登山地図 |publisher=竹田市 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。{{要出典範囲|「白水」という名は、鉱泉に含まれる多量の二酸化炭素のために、周囲の岩や土壌が白く染まり、湧き出す水が白く見えることに由来する。|date=2019年5月}}
鉱泉は、「ラムネ水」とも呼ばれて飲用とされ、砂糖を加えると[[サイダー]]に似た味となる<ref name="park" />。飲用は無料。また、持ち帰りは、500ｍｌペットボトル1本まで無料<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=白水鉱泉 |work=iナビおおいた |publisher=[[大分放送]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
入浴施設もあり、冷泉と鉱泉を沸かした湯とに入浴できる。源泉温度は、9℃で、心臓弁膜症、心筋炎、貧血症、更年期障害に効能があるとされる<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.e-obs.com/blog/onsen_sommelier/?p=3374 |title=白水鉱泉 黒嶽荘 |work=OBSラジオ「なみじゅんの温泉ソムリエ」 |publisher=大分放送 |date=2016-04-01 |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
西方には、[[名水百選]]に選定された[[男池湧水群]]が位置している<ref name="park" />。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 白水（しらみず）鉱泉] - 日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト
{{Pref-stub|pref=大分県}} {{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらみすこうせん}} [[Category:大分県の温泉]] [[Category:由布市の地理]]

{{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water}} '''白水鉱泉'''（しらみずこうせん）は、[[大分県]]中南部の[[由布市]][[庄内町 (大分県)|庄内町]]阿蘇野にある[[鉱泉]]。日本では珍しい純粋な天然[[炭酸水]]の鉱泉（単純[[二酸化炭素泉]]）として有名である<ref name="visit-oita">{{cite web |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=白水（しらみず）鉱泉 |work=日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト |publisher=[[ツーリズムおおいた]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{cite web |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=国立公園へ出かけよう！ くじゅう_おすすめコース 男池・白水鉱泉コース |publisher=環境省 |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
[[九重山|九重連山]]東部に位置する[[黒岳 (大分県)|黒岳]]（標高1,586 [[メートル|m]]）の山麓にあり<ref name="visit-oita" />、黒岳への登山口でもある<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=くじゅう連山登山地図 |publisher=竹田市 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。{{要出典範囲|「白水」という名は、鉱泉に含まれる多量の二酸化炭素のために、周囲の岩や土壌が白く染まり、湧き出す水が白く見えることに由来する。|date=2019年5月}}
鉱泉は、「ラムネ水」とも呼ばれて飲用とされ、砂糖を加えると[[サイダー]]に似た味となる<ref name="park" />。飲用は無料。また、持ち帰りは、500ｍｌペットボトル1本まで無料<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=白水鉱泉 |work=iナビおおいた |publisher=[[大分放送]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
入浴施設もあり、冷泉と鉱泉を沸かした湯とに入浴できる。源泉温度は、9℃で、心臓弁膜症、心筋炎、貧血症、更年期障害に効能があるとされる<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.e-obs.com/blog/onsen_sommelier/?p=3374 |title=白水鉱泉 黒嶽荘 |work=OBSラジオ「なみじゅんの温泉ソムリエ」 |publisher=大分放送 |date=2016-04-01 |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
西方には、[[名水百選]]に選定された[[男池湧水群]]が位置している<ref name="park" />。
2019年、白水鉱泉のある、庄内町阿蘇野地区周辺の飲泉文化を含む、庄内町の温泉文化が「[[庄内温泉]]」として、[[国民保養温泉地]]「[[湯布院温泉郷]]」の一つに認定された<ref name=":1">{{Cite news|title=由布市一体で「湯布院温泉郷」-「由布院」「湯平」「塚原」「庄内」「挾間」 国民保養温泉地に指定|date=2019-9-7|newspaper=大分合同新聞|url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851}}</ref>。

{{Pref-stub|pref=大分県}} {{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらみすこうせん}} [[Category:大分県の温泉]] [[Category:由布市の地理]]

{{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water}} '''白水鉱泉'''（しらみずこうせん）は、[[大分県]]中南部の[[由布市]][[庄内町 (大分県)|庄内町]]阿蘇野にある[[鉱泉]]。日本では珍しい純粋な天然[[炭酸水]]の鉱泉（単純[[二酸化炭素泉]]）として有名である<ref name="visit-oita">{{cite web |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=白水（しらみず）鉱泉 |work=日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト |publisher=[[ツーリズムおおいた]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{cite web |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=国立公園へ出かけよう！ くじゅう_おすすめコース 男池・白水鉱泉コース |publisher=環境省 |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
[[九重山|九重連山]]東部に位置する[[黒岳 (大分県)|黒岳]]（標高1,586 [[メートル|m]]）の山麓にあり<ref name="visit-oita" />、黒岳への登山口でもある<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=くじゅう連山登山地図 |publisher=竹田市 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。{{要出典範囲|「白水」という名は、鉱泉に含まれる多量の二酸化炭素のために、周囲の岩や土壌が白く染まり、湧き出す水が白く見えることに由来する。|date=2019年5月}}
鉱泉は、「ラムネ水」とも呼ばれて飲用とされ、砂糖を加えると[[サイダー]]に似た味となる<ref name="park" />。飲用は無料。また、持ち帰りは、500ｍｌペットボトル1本まで無料<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=白水鉱泉 |work=iナビおおいた |publisher=[[大分放送]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
入浴施設もあり、冷泉と鉱泉を沸かした湯とに入浴できる。源泉温度は、9℃で、心臓弁膜症、心筋炎、貧血症、更年期障害に効能があるとされる<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.e-obs.com/blog/onsen_sommelier/?p=3374 |title=白水鉱泉 黒嶽荘 |work=OBSラジオ「なみじゅんの温泉ソムリエ」 |publisher=大分放送 |date=2016-04-01 |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
西方には、[[名水百選]]に選定された[[男池湧水群]]が位置している<ref name="park" />。
2019年10月4日、従来「[[湯布院温泉]]」として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定されていた[[由布院温泉]]、[[湯平温泉]]に、庄内町阿蘇野地区周辺の飲泉を含む[[庄内温泉]]等の3地域の温泉を加えた5つの地域の温泉が「[[湯布院温泉郷]]」として国民保養温泉地に拡充指定された<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=由布市一体で「湯布院温泉郷」 |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=湯布院温泉郷（由布院温泉、湯平温泉、塚原温泉、庄内温泉、挾間温泉）国民保養温泉地計画書 |publisher=由布市 |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 白水（しらみず）鉱泉] - 日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト
{{Pref-stub|pref=大分県}} {{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらみすこうせん}} [[Category:大分県の温泉]] [[Category:由布市の地理]]

revision.comment: Created page with "== Natural Carbon Dioxide Springs == [[File:RamuneOnsenExterior.JPG|thumb|Lamune Onsenkan (Nagayu Onsen)]] Carbon Dioxide Springs are hot springs with a high concentration of dissolved carbon dioxide, which are rare worldwide. The {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉}} area, home to {{ill|Lamune Onsenkan|ja|ラムネ温泉館}}, has an abundance of natural Carbon Dioxide Springs. There are relatively few natural Carbon Dioxide Springs in Japan. Even fewer are pure Carbon D..."
revision.text:
== Natural Carbon Dioxide Springs == [[File:RamuneOnsenExterior.JPG|thumb|Lamune Onsenkan (Nagayu Onsen)]] Carbon Dioxide Springs are hot springs with a high concentration of dissolved carbon dioxide, which are rare worldwide. The {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉}} area, home to {{ill|Lamune Onsenkan|ja|ラムネ温泉館}}, has an abundance of natural Carbon Dioxide Springs. There are relatively few natural Carbon Dioxide Springs in Japan. Even fewer are pure Carbon Dioxide Springs that do not contain {{ill|sodium bicarbonate|ja|炭酸水素ナトリウム}} or {{ill|salt|ja|食塩}}. One example is {{ill|Shiramizu Mineral Spring|ja|白水鉱泉}} in Ōita Prefecture, which is a nearly pure Simple Carbon Dioxide Spring. Another is Nagayu Onsen, which has both Simple Carbon Dioxide Springs and Bicarbonate Springs.
Japan has few Carbon Dioxide Springs due to its high volcanic activity, which results in high-temperature springs where carbon dioxide is less soluble. Outside Japan, there are many Carbon Dioxide Springs in {{ill|Europe|ja|ヨーロッパ}}, especially in {{ill|Germany|ja|ドイツ}}, where well-known hot spring resorts such as {{ill|Baden-Baden|ja|バーデン＝バーデン}} and {{ill|Bad Nauheim|ja|バート・ナウハイム}} exist.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.co2spa.com/about/ |title=What is a Carbon Dioxide Spring? |publisher=Mitsubishi Chemical Aqua Solutions |accessdate=2021-09-12 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.co2spa.com/history/ |title=History of Carbon Dioxide Springs |publisher=Mitsubishi Chemical Aqua Solutions |accessdate=2021-09-12 }}</ref>.

=== Notable Hot Springs in Japan === * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長湯温泉]]<ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:船小屋温泉]]<ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7"/> (Fukuoka Prefecture) [[Category:Carbon Dioxide Springs]][[Category:Carbon Dioxide Springs forks]]

One example is {{ill|Shiramizu Mineral Spring|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:白水鉱泉]] in Ōita Prefecture, which is a nearly pure Simple Carbon Dioxide Spring. Another is Nagayu Onsen, which has both Simple Carbon Dioxide Springs and Bicarbonate Springs.
Japan has few Carbon Dioxide Springs due to its high volcanic activity, which results in high-temperature springs where carbon dioxide is less soluble. Outside Japan, there are many Carbon Dioxide Springs in {{ill|Europe|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:ヨーロッパ]], especially in {{ill|Germany|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:ドイツ]], where well-known hot spring resorts such as {{ill|Baden-Baden|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:バーデン＝バーデン]] and {{ill|Bad Nauheim|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:バート・ナウハイム]] exist.<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.co2spa.com/about/ |title=What is a Carbon Dioxide Spring? |publisher=Mitsubishi Chemical Aqua Solutions |accessdate=2021-09-12 ]]</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.co2spa.com/history/ |title=History of Carbon Dioxide Springs |publisher=Mitsubishi Chemical Aqua Solutions |accessdate=2021-09-12 ]]</ref>.
=== Notable Hot Springs in Japan === * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:長湯温泉]]<ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|en}}[[Category:new pages]][[ja:船小屋温泉]]<ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7"/> (Fukuoka Prefecture) [[Category:Carbon Dioxide Springs]][[Category:Carbon Dioxide Springs forks]] [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a {{ill|mineral spring|ja|鉱泉}} located in {{ill|Yufu, Ōita|ja|由布市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural {{ill|carbonated water|ja|炭酸水}} spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|ja|九重山}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|ja|九重山}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}}

{{Pref-stub|pref=Ōita Prefecture}} {{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E|display=title}} [[Category:Hot springs in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Yufu, Ōita]] [[ja:白水鉱泉]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[Category: 2]] [[ja:白水鉱泉]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[Category: 2]] [[ja:ヨーロッパ]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[Category: 2]] [[ja:ドイツ]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[Category: 2]] [[ja:バーデン＝バーデン]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[Category: 2]] [[ja:バート・ナウハイム]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[Category: 2]] [[ja:長湯温泉]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[Category: 2]] [[ja:船小屋温泉]] [[Category:Carbon Dioxide Springs]] [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a {{ill|mineral spring|ja|鉱泉|1=Mineral spring}} located in {{ill|Yufu, Ōita|ja|由布市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural {{ill|carbonated water|ja|炭酸水}} spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|ja|九重山}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}}

{{Pref-stub|pref=Ōita Prefecture}} {{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E|display=title}} [[Category:Hot springs in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Yufu, Ōita]] [[ja:白水鉱泉]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

[[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:白水鉱泉]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:ヨーロッパ]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:ドイツ]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:バーデン＝バーデン]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:バート・ナウハイム]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:長湯温泉]] [[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:船小屋温泉]] [[Category:Carbon Dioxide Springs]] [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a {{ill|mineral spring|ja|鉱泉|1=Mineral spring}} located in {{ill|Yufu, Ōita|ja|由布市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural {{ill|carbonated water|ja|炭酸水}} spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|ja|九重山}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}}

[[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:白水鉱泉]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:ヨーロッパ]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:ドイツ]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:バーデン＝バーデン]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:バート・ナウハイム]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:長湯温泉]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:船小屋温泉]] [[Category:Carbon Dioxide Springs]] [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a {{ill|mineral spring|ja|鉱泉|1=Mineral spring}} located in {{ill|Yufu, Ōita|ja|由布市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural {{ill|carbonated water|ja|炭酸水}} spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|ja|九重山}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}}== See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉}} [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:長湯温泉]]<ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉}} [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:not indeterminate]][[ja:船小屋温泉]]<ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural {{ill|carbonated water|ja|炭酸水}} spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|ja|九重山}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=draft}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=draft}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=draft}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=draft}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=draft}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=draft}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|二酸化炭素泉|" to "|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|"
revision.text:
[[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a {{ill|mineral spring|ja|鉱泉|1=Mineral spring|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yufu, Ōita|ja|由布市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural {{ill|carbonated water|ja|炭酸水|12=draft}} spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=draft}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|ja|九重山|12=draft}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=draft}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=draft}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=draft}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=draft}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=draft}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=draft}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=draft}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs] - Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Official Site
{{Pref-stub|pref=Ōita Prefecture}} {{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E|display=title}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:Carbon Dioxide Springs]] [[Category:Hot springs in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Yufu, Ōita]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:白水鉱泉]] [[ja:ヨーロッパ]] [[ja:ドイツ]] [[ja:バーデン＝バーデン]] [[ja:バート・ナウハイム]] [[ja:長湯温泉]] [[ja:船小屋温泉]]

Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>

{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=draft}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=draft}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=draft}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=draft}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=draft}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
17 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:白水鉱泉 up to rev 102446440
revision.text:
[[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|白水鉱泉の社屋と取水場]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''白水鉱泉'''（しらみずこうせん）は、[[大分県]]中南部の[[由布市]][[庄内町阿蘇野]]にある[[鉱泉]]。日本では珍しい純粋な天然[[炭酸水]]の鉱泉（単純[[二酸化炭素泉]]）として有名である<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=白水（しらみず）鉱泉 |work=日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト |publisher=[[ツーリズムおおいた]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=国立公園へ出かけよう！ くじゅう_おすすめコース 男池・白水鉱泉コース |publisher=環境省 |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
[[九重山|九重連山]]東部に位置する[[黒岳 (大分県)|黒岳]]（標高1,586 [[メートル|m]]）の山麓にあり<ref name="visit-oita" />、黒岳への登山口でもある<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=くじゅう連山登山地図 |publisher=竹田市 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。{{要出典範囲|「白水」という名は、鉱泉に含まれる多量の二酸化炭素のために、周囲の岩や土壌が白く染まり、湧き出す水が白く見えることに由来する。|date=2019年5月}}
鉱泉は、「ラムネ水」とも呼ばれて飲用とされ、砂糖を加えると[[サイダー]]に似た味となる<ref name="park" />。飲用は無料。また、持ち帰りは、500ｍｌペットボトル1人1本まで無料<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=白水鉱泉 |work=iナビおおいた |publisher=[[大分放送]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。
西方には、[[名水百選]]に選定された[[男池湧水群]]が位置している<ref name="park" />。
2019年10月4日、従来「[[湯布院温泉]]」として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定されていた[[由布院温泉]]、[[湯平温泉]]に、庄内町阿蘇野地区周辺の飲泉を含む[[庄内温泉]]等の3地域の温泉を加えた5つの地域の温泉が「[[湯布院温泉郷]]」として国民保養温泉地に拡充指定された<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=由布市一体で「湯布院温泉郷」 |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=湯布院温泉郷（由布院温泉、湯平温泉、塚原温泉、庄内温泉、挾間温泉）国民保養温泉地計画書 |publisher=由布市 |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>。 {{-}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 白水（しらみず）鉱泉] - 日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト
{{Pref-stub|pref=大分県}} {{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらみすこうせん}} [[Category:大分県の温泉]] [[Category:由布市の地理]]

[[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|白水鉱泉の社屋と取水場]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''白水鉱泉'''（しらみずこうせん）は、[[大分県]]中南部の[[由布市]][[庄内町阿蘇野]]にある[[鉱泉]]。日本では珍しい純粋な天然[[炭酸水]]の鉱泉（単純[[二酸化炭素泉]]）として有名である<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=白水（しらみず）鉱泉 |work=日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト |publisher=[[ツーリズムおおいた]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=国立公園へ出かけよう！ くじゅう_おすすめコース 男池・白水鉱泉コース |publisher=環境省 |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>。

西方には、[[名水百選]]に選定された[[男池湧水群]]が位置している<ref name="park" />。
2019年10月4日、従来「[[湯布院温泉]]」として[[国民保養温泉地]]に指定されていた[[由布院温泉]]、[[湯平温泉]]に、庄内町阿蘇野地区周辺の飲泉を含む[[庄内温泉]]等の3地域の温泉を加えた5つの地域の温泉が「[[湯布院温泉郷]]」として国民保養温泉地に拡充指定された<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=由布市一体で「湯布院温泉郷」 |newspaper=大分合同新聞 |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=湯布院温泉郷（由布院温泉、湯平温泉、塚原温泉、庄内温泉、挾間温泉）国民保養温泉地計画書 |publisher=由布市 |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>。 {{-}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 白水（しらみず）鉱泉] - 日本一の「おんせん県」大分県の観光情報公式サイト

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|qq=|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|qq=|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|qq=|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|qq=|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|qq=|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|qq=|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|qq=|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|qq=|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|qq=|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|qq=|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|qq=|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|qq=|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|qq=|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|qq=|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

{{wikidata link|Q11579917}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)
