<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>

To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a [[:en:Mineral spring|Mineral spring]] located in [[:en:Yufu|Yufu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural [[:en:Carbonated water|carbonated water]] spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of [[:en:Mount Kujū|Mount Kujū]], on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11579917]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a {{ill|Mineral spring|en|Mineral spring|WD=Q1365924|lt=Mineral spring}} located in {{ill|Yufu|en|Yufu|WD=Q990455|lt=Yufu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural {{ill|Carbonated water|en|Carbonated water|WD=Q264554|lt=carbonated water}} spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|en|Mount Kujū|WD=Q6921721|lt=Mount Kujū}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]]
[[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a {{ill|Mineral spring|en|Mineral spring|WD=Q1365924|lt=Mineral spring}} located in {{ill|Yufu|en|Yufu|WD=Q990455|lt=Yufu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural {{ill|Carbonated water|en|Carbonated water|WD=Q264554|lt=carbonated water}} spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|en|Mount Kujū|WD=Q6921721|lt=Mount Kujū}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
{{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Nagayu Onsen|ja|長湯温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayu Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7">Yamamura Junji, ''47 Prefectures: Hot Spring Encyclopedia'', p.7, Maruzen Publishing, 2015, ISBN 978-4-621-08996-5</ref> (Ōita Prefecture) * {{ill|Funagoya Onsen|ja|船小屋温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funagoya Onsen|qq=}} <ref name="Yamamura_47_h_p7" /> (Fukuoka Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白水鉱泉]]
[[File:Hakusui mineral springs.jpg|280px|thumb|Hakusui Mineral Springs facility and water collection area]] {{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|type=point|coord={{Coord|33|07|06.3|N|131|18|49.6|E}}|marker=water|220px}} '''Hakusui Mineral Springs''' (白水鉱泉) is a {{ill|Mineral spring|en|Mineral spring|WD=Q1365924|lt=Mineral spring|1=Mineral spring}} located in {{ill|Yufu|en|Yufu|WD=Q990455|lt=Yufu, Ōita|1=Yufu}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, in the central-southern region of the prefecture. It is known as a rare example in Japan of a natural {{ill|Carbonated water|en|Carbonated water|WD=Q264554|lt=carbonated water|1=Carbonated water}} spring with high purity (classified as a simple {{ill|carbon dioxide spring|ja|二酸化炭素泉|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|1=Carbon Dioxide Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/carbon dioxide spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Carbon Dioxide Springs|qq=}}).<ref name="visit-oita">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.visit-oita.jp/spots/detail/5594 |title=Hakusui (Shiramizu) Mineral Springs |work=Japan's No.1 "Onsen Prefecture" Ōita Prefecture Tourism Information Official Site |publisher=[[Tourism Ōita]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref><ref name="park">{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.env.go.jp/park/guide/kuju/recommend/02.html |title=Let's Visit a National Park! Kuju - Recommended Course: Oike & Hakusui Mineral Springs Route |publisher=Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
The spring is located at the eastern foot of {{ill|Mount Kujū|en|Mount Kujū|WD=Q6921721|lt=Mount Kujū|1=Mount Kujū}}, on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|ja|黒岳 (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurodake (Ōita)|1=Mount Kuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678141}} (elevation 1,586 m).<ref name="visit-oita" /> It also serves as an entrance to hiking trails leading up Mount Kurodake.<ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=https://www.city.taketa.oita.jp/sightseeing/nature/pdf/tozanzu.pdf |title=Kuju Mountain Range Hiking Map |publisher=Taketa City |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
{{Citation needed|The name "Hakusui" (白水, meaning "white water") is derived from the fact that the high carbon dioxide content causes surrounding rocks and soil to turn white, giving the flowing water a whitish appearance.|date=May 2019}}
The mineral water, also known as "Ramune Water," is drinkable and takes on a taste similar to cider when mixed with sugar.<ref name="park" /> Drinking the water at the source is free of charge. Visitors can take home one 500ml PET bottle per person for free.<ref name="visit-oita" /><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.i-oita.net/spot/hot_springs/yufu/9756.html |title=Hakusui Mineral Springs |work=i-Navi Ōita |publisher=[[Ōita Broadcasting System]] |accessdate=2019-05-25 }}</ref>
To the west of Hakusui Mineral Springs is the {{ill|Oike Spring Group|ja|男池湧水群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oike Spring Group|1=Oike Springs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11578162}}, which has been designated as one of {{ill|Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|ja|名水百選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s 100 Famous Waters|1=Selected 100 Exquisite and Well-Conserved Waters of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10917783}}.<ref name="park" />
On October 4, 2019, the area surrounding Hakusui Mineral Springs, including the drinking springs in the Asono district of Shōnai Town, was added to the {{ill|Yufuin Onsen Village|ja|湯布院温泉郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen Village|1=Yufuin Onsenkyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86732501}} as part of an expansion of the {{ill|National Health Resort|ja|国民保養温泉地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Health Resort|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}} designation. Previously, only {{ill|Yufuin Onsen|ja|由布院温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yufuin Onsen|1=Yufuin Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11264855}} and {{ill|Yunohira Onsen|ja|湯平温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunohira Onsen|1=Yunohira Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563564}} were included under the "Yufuin Onsen" designation.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2019/09/07/JD0058455851 |title=Yufu City as a Whole Designated as "Yufuin Onsen Village" |newspaper=Ōita Gōdō Shimbun |date=2019-09-07 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.city.yufu.oita.jp/wp-content/uploads/2019/10/hoyouonsentikeikakusho.pdf |title=Yufuin Onsen Village (Yufuin Onsen, Yunohira Onsen, Tsukahara Onsen, Shōnai Onsen, Hasama Onsen) National Health Resort Plan |publisher=Yufu City |date=2019-10-04 |format=PDF |accessdate=2019-10-10 }}</ref>
